name: Fuente del Buey Local Trail
name_original: Alto de los Pozuelos y Fuente del Buey – running circular desde Frías de Albarracín
description: This moderate 8.1-mile jogging circular from Frías de Albarracín offers varied terrain and views from Alto de los Pozuelos.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Frías De Albarracín often traverse varied landscapes, including river valleys, forested areas, and elevated viewpoints. The region is characterized by its natural springs, such as the Ojos del Cabriel, and numerous local trails that offer a mix of terrain. Runners can expect paths that wind through woodlands and open areas, with some routes featuring moderate elevation changes. Best jogging routes around Frías De Albarracín The most popular jogging route is Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall, a 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes about 54 minutes to complete. This route explores the springs of the Cabriel river and a notable waterfall. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.7 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from the Alto de las Peñas viewpoint. Local runners also love the Fuente del Buey Local Trail, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through natural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Jogging around Frías De Albarracín is defined by river valleys, forested paths, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more challenging trails with elevation gain. More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Frías De Albarracín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Frías De Albarracín?
A: There are 14 running routes documented in Frías De Albarracín, offering a variety of experiences for joggers. These include 10 moderate routes and 4 more challenging options.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Frías De Albarracín?
A: While there are no routes officially classified as 'easy' for beginners, many of the 10 moderate routes offer accessible terrain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983156660/ojos-del-cabriel-and-cascada-del-molino-de-san-pedro-circular-by-la-herreria-waterfall" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option at 4.6 miles (7.5 km) that explores natural springs and a waterfall.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Frías De Albarracín?
A: Running routes in Frías De Albarracín often traverse varied landscapes, including picturesque river valleys, dense forested areas, and elevated viewpoints. You'll find paths winding through woodlands and open spaces, with many routes featuring moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there challenging running routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there are 4 routes classified as difficult. These trails will provide more significant elevation gain, testing your endurance amidst the region's hilly terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983315048/fuente-del-buey-local-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Buey Local Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features over 235 meters of ascent over its 8.1 miles (13.0 km).

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Frías De Albarracín?
A: Many of the running routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Popular circular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983323510/mirador-del-alto-de-las-penas-circular-from-terriente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983274448/guadalaviar-griegos-circular-running-by-the-fuente-coveta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar - Griegos - circular running by the Fuente Coveta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the running routes?
A: The running routes often pass by beautiful natural attractions. You can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt; (springs of the Cabriel river), the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/604533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also lead through the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Mingo Marco&lt;/a&gt; gorge.

Q: Are there any viewpoints accessible from the running trails?
A: Yes, several running routes offer access to scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983323510/mirador-del-alto-de-las-penas-circular-from-terriente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase panoramic vistas from the Alto de las Peñas viewpoint, making it a great choice for runners who appreciate a good view.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Frías De Albarracín?
A: Runners frequently praise the varied terrain, which includes river valleys, forested paths, and scenic viewpoints. The region's natural springs and waterfalls are also highlights, providing beautiful backdrops for a run. Over 60 runners have used komoot to explore these diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in the area?
A: While most routes are moderate in length, there are options for longer runs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983315048/fuente-del-buey-local-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Buey Local Trail&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longer options, covering 8.1 miles (13.0 km) through natural landscapes, ideal for those looking to extend their run.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass through or near villages?
A: Yes, some routes connect to or pass near local settlements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983274448/guadalaviar-griegos-circular-running-by-the-fuente-coveta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar - Griegos - circular running by the Fuente Coveta&lt;/a&gt; route includes the village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos&lt;/a&gt;, offering a chance to experience the local charm.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the running paths?
A: Yes, you might encounter historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de las Pisadas&lt;/a&gt;, a historical mill, is one such site that can be found in the vicinity of some running trails, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Is there a route that features the Pino Monumental?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983328144/pino-monumental-running-circular-from-moscardon-through-prado-molinero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pino Monumental – running circular from Moscardón through Prado Molinero&lt;/a&gt; is a specific route designed to take you past this notable natural monument, offering an 8.8-mile (8.9 km) run through varied terrain.
tips: Panoramic point a few meters from the forest track with very nice views towards the valley of Frías de Albarracín. It allows to give a very precise idea of the beautiful landscape of the area of the Montes Universales and what is known as the Spanish Lapland.

Recreation area and picnic area with tables, grills and a water fountain located in the middle of the Montes Universales. An amazing place with beautiful views and very quiet, ideal to rest and enjoy nature and silence.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Alto de los Pozuelos y Fuente del Buey circular route?
faqs.answer: For an average jogger, this route usually takes around 1 hour and 29 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how long you spend enjoying the viewpoints.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied experience. Approximately 8.6 km consists of loose ground, while about 4.5 km is asphalt. You'll encounter a mix of general paths, roads, and hiking trails. While some sections are smooth, others on loose ground and hiking trails may require more careful footing due to potentially rocky or uneven surfaces. Parts of the dirt track are wide, well-signposted, and in good condition.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Alto de los Pozuelos y Fuente del Buey running circular?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Frías de Albarracín. You should be able to find suitable parking within the village to begin your run.

faqs.question: Is the Alto de los Pozuelos y Fuente del Buey running circular suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate in difficulty. While it's a rewarding experience, its length of 13 km and elevation gain of around 235 meters mean it requires good physical condition, especially if you plan to jog the entire route. Beginners with some running experience might find it challenging but achievable.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. The Alto de los Pozuelos viewpoint offers beautiful views of the Frías de Albarracín valley and the Montes Universales. You'll also pass by the Fuente del Buey, a recreational area with tables, grills, and a natural water fountain, perfect for a break. The route also goes through a Special Protection Area for Birds (Z.E.P.A.) and near the Fuente del Enebro, known for amphibians.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trails in the Frías de Albarracín area, including this circular route, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through areas with wildlife or near other visitors.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or need to pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this trail or the surrounding area of Frías de Albarracín. However, please respect the natural environment, especially as parts of the route pass through a Special Protection Area for Birds (Z.E.P.A.).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Albarracín region experiences distinct seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Summer can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon run is advisable. Winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow, which would add to the challenge and require appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Albarracín - Nacimiento del Tajo trail (covering about 34% of the route) and the Barranco de la Hoz trail (covering about 2%).

faqs.question: What should I bring for a run on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing appropriate running shoes with good grip, sufficient water, and snacks. Depending on the weather, layers of clothing are advisable. A phone for navigation and emergencies is also a good idea, as is sun protection.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to refuel along the way?
faqs.answer: The Fuente del Buey is a significant recreational area along the route, offering tables, grills, and a natural water fountain. This makes it an ideal spot to take a break, refill your water bottle, or enjoy a snack during or after your run.
